summary: Fiery Jack Straw opener, tight jams, Phil’s bass shines
blurb: The show opens with a scorching Jack Straw and rolls through a high‑energy first set. Highlights include a soaring Cassidy, a superb Space→Truckin' transition and an inspired Miracle (I Need A Miracle) in the second set. Phil’s bass is front‑and‑center throughout, making this a must‑listen Red Rocks performance.
review: September 7, 1983 at Red Rocks captured the Dead in top form. The first set bursts out with Jack Straw and a punchy Candyman, while Loser showcases Jerry’s bright, energetic tone – reviewers note he “leaves it all onstage.” A tight segued China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider keeps the momentum flowing into a second‑set that balances classic peaks (Bertha, Space→Truckin') with an unexpected Miracle during I Need A Miracle, where Jerry’s “wicked licks” shine. Phil’s bass is unusually prominent, described as “bombs” and “joyful,” anchoring the band’s groove across both sets. Though a few audience recordings mention occasional vocal distortion from Bob, the overall mix—especially the balanced soundboard (gd1983‑09‑07.nak300.morris…)—delivers clear instrument separation and a crisp capture of the night’s high energy.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings carry positive sentiment, repeatedly praising the performance (e.g., "strong 83 show," "fiery Jack Straw," "standout Space→Truckin'"), highlighting multiple songs and consistent praise for Phil’s bass presence and Jerry’s energetic playing. No negative comments about musical quality were noted, just minor recording quirks.

best_recording.reason: Balanced tonal quality, clear mix and prominent Phil bass make it the clearest representation of the show.

key_highlights: Jack Straw opens with fiery intensity and bright Garcia tone
Space → Truckin' jam stands out as a highlight of the second set
I Need A Miracle (Miracle) features wicked Jerry licks
Phil’s bass sits front‑and‑center, delivering joyful "bombs" throughout

band_performance.Jerry: Energetic and bright; leaves it all onstage, especially on Loser, Space and the Miracle section of I Need A Miracle.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is prominent and joyful, described as "bombs"; provides a solid groove that drives the whole show.
band_performance.Bob: Generally solid rhythm work, though a few reviewers note occasional vocal/guitar distortion on several songs.
